title: Podman network fails dns randomly
body:
### Issue Description

Podman's network fails DNS after some time. Ping works on host with domain name and ip, ping fails inside container with domain name, but succeeds with ip.
Have had this issue a few times atleast, all recent. The temporary solution I think is to kill the existing aarvark-dns process then bring down the container and recreate.
Maybe related I recently upgraded to podman 6.0.0.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Im not sure if this is the most accurate description, but it should not be an error with the docker compose below since I had this issue across another compose stack on the same machine which was using a php image.

1. I just bring up a compose app (example below)
2. Try to do dns queries. (Original error was package installs were failing)

```
services:
  app:
    depends_on:
      - db
    image: oven/bun:1.3-alpine
    volumes:
      - .:/home/bun/app
    env_file:
      - .env
    command: tail -f /dev/null
    # command: sh -c " \
    #   bun install --frozen-lockfile && \
    #   bun run dev"
    # command: bun run seed
```

3. The tests I tried
```
(inside container)
/home/bun/app # ping google.com
ping: bad address 'google.com'
/home/bun/app # exit
Error: executing /usr/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose exec -it app sh: exit status 1

~/Projects/ams-v3 master* 54s
❯ ping google.com
PING google.com (74.125.24.138) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from sf-in-f138.1e100.net (74.125.24.138): icmp_seq=1 ttl=108 time=58.9 ms
^C
--- google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 58.933/58.933/58.933/0.000 ms

~/Projects/ams-v3 master*
❯ podman compose exec -it app ping 74.125.24.138
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

PING 74.125.24.138 (74.125.24.138): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 74.125.24.138: seq=0 ttl=42 time=77.495 ms
^C
--- 74.125.24.138 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 77.495/77.495/77.495 ms

~/Projects/ams-v3 master*
❯ podman compose exec -it app ping google.com
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

ping: bad address 'google.com'
Error: executing /usr/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose exec -it app ping google.com: exit status 1

~/Projects/ams-v3 master*
❯
```


### Describe the results you received

Package installs failed / pings with domain names failed, but those with IPs succeed.

### Describe the results you expected

It should work. (even with domain name)

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

CachyOS

### Additional information

Has happened even after a couple restarts. The first few podman compose up's will be ok, but after a while it will show this error.

comments.body:
From my testing i found the internal dns would respond for other containers inside the compose (havent tried pinging other containers on same machine), but it would fail for public links like google.com.
Manually adding extra dns works as a band aid for now.
```
services:
  app:
    # depends_on:
    #   - db
    image: oven/bun:1.3-alpine
    volumes:
      - .:/home/bun/app
    env_file:
      - .env
    working_dir: /home/bun/app
    # command: tail -f /dev/null
    command: sh -c " \
      bun install --frozen-lockfile && \
      bun run dev"
    # command: bun run seed
    dns:
      - 1.1.1.1
      - 8.8.8.8
```

Would this be an issue in podman or docker compose or?

I assume you run rootless? When this happens what is the output of ` podman unshare --rootless-netns sh -c "ip addr; ip route; cat /etc/resolv.conf"`?

If local dns still works and manually adding the global dns it sounds liek the custom rootless-netns ends up with a unusable nameserver. For rootless we rely on the pasta --dns-forward proxy ip we set and that istels reads the /etc/resolv.conf on the host on startup to get the namesever. 

Do you have the containers starting on boot via systemd? I guess it is likely that they start to early before the network configuration is done. Stopping aardvark-dns manually should not be required, to recreate the rootless-netns you need to stop all containers first and then start them again this should create a new namespace with a anew pasta and aardvark-dns process that pick up the current network config.

This is rootless podman with systemd. 
I have tried to recreate this issue over the past few days, but after my report I havent been able to... I will try a few more times but either way should I close this issue?

> Maybe related I recently upgraded to podman 6.0.0.

I think I recently experienced the same issue on v5.8. All of a sudden (after being up for weeks) DNS stopped working inside containers but worked on the host machine.

> This is rootless podman with systemd.

I also run rootless with systemd.

> I will try a few more times but either way should I close this issue?

I think the issue should not be closed until it's resolved since it seems it's not just an issue on your end.

> Do you have the containers starting on boot via systemd? I guess it is likely that they start to early before the network configuration is done.

1. In my case the DNS issue appeared suddenly after the host machine and the containers had been up for weeks. If the issue was caused by an incorrect configuration startup order, wouldn't it be present immediately after bootup?
2. I thought configuring the container startup on boot via systemd is the standard way to do so. What are systemd users supposed to do if it doesn't work as it should?

I tried running `podman network reload --all` and got the following error for each container:
```
[ERROR netavark::dns::aardvark] aardvark-dns runs in a different netns, dns will not work for this container. To resolve please stop all containers, kill the aardvark-dns process, remove the /run/user/<uid>/containers/networks/aardvark-dns directory and then start the containers again
```

I have gotten the DNS error once again, though I do not recall restarting in a while. 
Network reload output:
```
❯ podman network reload --all

4fbba13cee3cf28491a3cdd84dd6f1f32ff080d9967ba4a2d7749f2080db4989

b9bb4500fda4a25f0335e7f2fb71d9002e07339161a0e5a2478c4670ccba74af
096b7e0252bc434f91030133ce368af8268888ea901f84e02fc708f3993bc791
bbf61f376ef3520247908b8f88ab6ee413f4d631d012557a33f140198c88eb3a
9851f37162c30564159d5ad59841391a36c80cf8f581bf047e9d707735c0afed
```
Did not give any errors but the issue still remained.
Also here's there output for the printing resolv.conf

Will also mention that bringing down all the containers and then bringing them up did fix it, however this is not the best solution.
